content.claim: Municipalities reported cost-saving as the primary rationale for rural school closures in Finland, citing per-pupil costs that can be twice as high in smaller schools, but few rigorous follow-up studies have verified whether municipalities actually realized claimed savings.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Stated rationale documented; evidentiary gap explicitly flagged between justification and verified fiscal outcome
No data on opportunity costs (e.g., transport costs, teacher retraining) or indirect savings claims
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
